title: task automatic called within fork within for loop: invalid argument
body:
```systemverilog
`timescale 1ns / 1ps

module dut_tb;

    logic clk;

    initial begin
        clk = 0;
        forever #(1) clk = ~clk;
    end

    task automatic send_req_packets(int module_id, int channel);
        $display("send_req_packets start:  module %0d, channel %0d", module_id, channel);
        @(posedge clk);
        $display("send_req_packets end:  module %0d, channel %0d", module_id, channel);
    endtask

    initial begin
        for (int m = 0; m < 2; m++) begin
            for (int i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin
                automatic int mod = m;
                automatic int ch = i;
                fork
                    send_req_packets(mod, ch);
                join_none
            end
        end

        repeat (10) @(posedge clk);

        $finish;
    end

endmodule

```
Verilator test Output
```
send_req_packets start:  module 0, channel 0
send_req_packets start:  module 0, channel 1
send_req_packets start:  module 1, channel 0
send_req_packets start:  module 1, channel 1
send_req_packets end:  module 1, channel 1
send_req_packets end:  module 1, channel 1
send_req_packets end:  module 1, channel 1
send_req_packets end:  module 1, channel 1

```

What 'verilator' command line do we use to run your example?
```
verilator -cc --binary --build -j 0 -sv --timescale 1ns/10ps --Mdir tb_verilator -o tb_verilator tb.sv  -timing
```
Then execute the binary


What 'verilator --version' are you using?  Did you try it with the git master version?
`Verilator 5.039 devel rev v5.038-36-g87050670b`

What OS and distribution are you using?
```
NAME="Red Hat Enterprise Linux"
VERSION="9.3 (Plow)"
```

May we assist you in trying to fix this in Verilator yourself?
I can try

If I run with xcelium I get
```
send_req_packets start:  module 1, channel 1
send_req_packets start:  module 1, channel 0
send_req_packets start:  module 0, channel 1
send_req_packets start:  module 0, channel 0
send_req_packets end:  module 1, channel 1
send_req_packets end:  module 1, channel 0
send_req_packets end:  module 0, channel 1
send_req_packets end:  module 0, channel 0
```
code_blocks:
`timescale 1ns / 1ps

module dut_tb;

    logic clk;

    initial begin
        clk = 0;
        forever #(1) clk = ~clk;
    end

    task automatic send_req_packets(int module_id, int channel);
        $display("send_req_packets start:  module %0d, channel %0d", module_id, channel);
        @(posedge clk);
        $display("send_req_packets end:  module %0d, channel %0d", module_id, channel);
    endtask

    initial begin
        for (int m = 0; m < 2; m++) begin
            for (int i = 0; i < 2; i++) begin
                automatic int mod = m;
                automatic int ch = i;
                fork
                    send_req_packets(mod, ch);
                join_none
            end
        end

        repeat (10) @(posedge clk);

        $finish;
    end

endmodule
